assertions.text: The vehicle drifted onto the shoulder (left or right unspecified) prior to contact with the trailer.
The vehicle made contact with the back (rear) of a parked trailer.
There was a single impact (one bump) with the parked trailer.
The collision was a light tap (minor severity).
The trailer was stationary/parked at the time of the collision.
After the bump, the truck remained driveable (could be driven away).
No braking is claimed or mentioned before the impact.

verdicts.evidence: Pre-impact steering shows a change with peak at [33.0](T2) degrees and baseline [7.5](T2) degrees indicating steering activity, but device axes/road shoulder direction are not documented so whether the vehicle drifted onto the shoulder (left or right) is unverifiable.

The EDR records a large longitudinal cumulative delta-V of [−98.2](T2) km/h (final value) with max of [−98.6](T2) km/h at [0.24](T2) s, indicating a rear or frontal longitudinal impact in device frame, but axis-to-vehicle-frame alignment and whether the struck object was a parked trailer rear face cannot be determined from sensors alone.

The crash pulse cumulative delta-V curve shows a large sustained change with final delta-V [−98.2](T2) km/h and peak [−98.6](T2) km/h at [0.24](T2) s, which is consistent with a major high-severity singular crash pulse rather than a single light bump; however sensors show only one primary pulse (num events cannot be detailed) so the small number of impact spikes is ambiguous — overall telemetry contradicts that this was a single light bump.

Severity is contradicted by the EDR summary which reports a large longitudinal max delta-V of [−99.1](T1) km/h (EDR summary) and the computed final cumulative delta-V of [−98.2](T2) km/h, values far above a light tap.

No sensor channel indicates the other vehicle/trailer motion or position; the telemetry only shows V1's motion (speed baseline [98.0](T2) to [96.0](T2) km/h) so whether the trailer was stationary/parked is unverifiable.

EDR shows engine speed and throttle channels exist but data do not include post-impact driveability; pre-impact rpm/throttle show operation but there is no post-impact running-state telemetry to prove the truck remained driveable, so this is unverifiable.

Brake channel samples in the pre-impact window show fraction on [0.36](T2) with the last sampled value [0](T2) at t=0.0 and a transition from on to off at [−2.75](T2) s, indicating no braking immediately before impact was recorded.

result.rationale: Telemetry shows a very large longitudinal delta-V ([−99.1](T1); final [−98.2](T2) km/h) inconsistent with a light bump; several claims about the trailer, drift direction, and driveability are unverifiable from V1 sensors alone.

uncited_quantitative_claims.context: window show fraction on   with the last sampled value   at t=0.0 and a transition from on to off at   s, indicating no braki

result.time_zero: impact (t=0); pre-crash negative, crash pulse positive

channels.name: delta_v_long
channels.desc: Cumulative longitudinal delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

result.notes: NHTSA CISS EDR data: sparse pre-crash series (~2 Hz, t<0) plus a crash-pulse delta-V curve (t>=0, ~10 ms steps). t=0 is the impact.

delta_v_long.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse
